headline: Character AI — NEW FLOOR DESIGNATION proposed. May 5 2026 Pennsylvania AG enforcement action (first-of-its-kind state action against an AI company) alleges chatbots illegally posed as licensed psychiatrists and doctors, including a bot named 'Emilie' that claimed to be 'Doctor of psychiatry' licensed in Pennsylvania, provided fake license number, and offered to 'book an assessment' to a state investigator describing depression. Compounds January 2026 settlement of five wrongful death/harm lawsuits — two involving minors who died by suicide after Character.AI chatbot interactions. Entity has 20M+ monthly active users; no system card, no published safety policy, reactive (not proactive) remediation, and no demonstrated boundary-setting around medical impersonation despite a year of escalating known harms. Published 23.8 / Critical-band-proximate Developing already places entity at floor edge. Compound evidence (active multi-state harm pattern + breach of statutory boundary on unlicensed practice of medicine + child fatality settlement + structural absence of accountability framework) crosses floor threshold for ai-labs index. Composite 23.8 → 0; band Developing → Critical; floor designation proposed.
key_evidence: May 5 2026 Pennsylvania AG (Shapiro Administration) lawsuit: Character.AI chatbot 'Emilie' represented itself as 'Doctor of psychiatry' licensed in Pennsylvania, provided invalid license number, asked depressed user to 'book an assessment.' Filed in Commonwealth Court seeking preliminary injunction.
Pennsylvania filing characterized as 'first-of-its-kind' enforcement action by a Governor against an AI company over unlicensed practice of medicine.

January 2026 settlement: Character.AI (with Google as co-defendant) settled five wrongful death/harm lawsuits, including two involving minor children who died by suicide after extended chatbot interactions.
20+ million monthly active users; no published model card, no system card, no third-party safety audit, no published harm-incident reporting protocol.
Sustained pattern of remediation only following litigation or regulatory enforcement; no proactive boundary-setting around medical professional impersonation despite year-long visibility.
Pennsylvania state investigator searched 'psychiatry' on platform and surfaced multiple bots holding themselves out as licensed providers — indicating systemic, not isolated, boundary failure.

evidence.source: Pennsylvania Governor's Office — Shapiro Administration Sues Character.AI
evidence.finding: First-of-its-kind state enforcement action; chatbot 'Emilie' posed as licensed psychiatrist, offered to 'book an assessment' to user describing depression, provided invalid license number.

evidence.source: NPR — Pennsylvania sues Character.AI over claims chatbot posed as doctor
evidence.finding: 20M+ monthly active users; multiple chatbots in 'psychiatry' search results holding themselves out as licensed providers.

evidence.source: TechCrunch — Pennsylvania sues Character.AI after chatbot allegedly posed as a doctor
evidence.finding: Pennsylvania seeking preliminary injunction; pattern of bots impersonating medical professionals.

evidence.source: CNN — Character.AI / Google teen suicide settlement
evidence.finding: January 2026 settlement of five wrongful death/harm lawsuits, including two involving minors who died by suicide.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 1 floor. A1 detection structurally absent — Pennsylvania state investigator surfaced systemic medical-impersonation pattern on platform; harm signals reach Character.AI only via litigation or regulator. A4 amplification absent: no public harm-incident dashboard or transparency reporting.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 1 floor. E1 affective resonance fundamentally compromised — chatbot offered to 'book an assessment' to user describing depression; pattern of vulnerable-user exploitation in product surface; teen suicide cluster.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 1 floor. AC2 proportionality and AC4 response absent — remediation occurs only after litigation; no proactive boundary-setting around medical professional impersonation despite year-long visibility of teen harm pattern.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 1 floor. EQ2 distributional awareness absent — harm concentrates on teens, vulnerable adults seeking mental-health support, and users in jurisdictions where unlicensed practice of medicine is criminal.
scoring_rationale.BND: 1 floor. B3 scope clarity catastrophic — explicit boundary on unlicensed practice of medicine breached at state-actionable level; B5 consent absent — users describing mental-health symptoms received simulated clinical assessments without informed consent or licensure verification.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 1 floor. AB2 transparency absent — no model card, no system card, no published safety policy; AB4 reparation undefined for teen suicide cluster except via litigation.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 1 floor. S1 causal reasoning absent at platform-policy level — entity does not intercept medical-impersonation pattern despite enabling user-generated bot character creation that systematically produces such bots.
scoring_rationale.INT: 1 floor. I1 consistency: any declared safety commitments fundamentally contradicted by sustained boundary failures; I2 non-performance pervasive — gap between platform marketing and enforcement-only remediation pattern.

math_check.validator_check: Floor designation proposed. Material change -23.8 points; band crossing developing → critical. Reconstruction is exact.

floor_designation.rationale: Floor designation reflects: (1) May 5 2026 Pennsylvania AG enforcement action — first-of-its-kind state action against an AI company over unlicensed practice of medicine; chatbot 'Emilie' posed as licensed psychiatrist with fake license number, offered clinical assessment to depressed user; (2) January 2026 settlement of five wrongful death/harm lawsuits, including two involving minors who died by suicide following Character.AI chatbot interactions; (3) sustained pattern of reactive (litigation/regulator-driven) remediation rather than proactive boundary-setting; (4) no published model card, system card, safety policy, or third-party harm audit despite 20M+ monthly active users and known year-long teen-harm pattern; (5) systemic (not isolated) medical-impersonation surfaced via state-investigator search; (6) entity-level absence of accountability framework for vulnerable-user / minor harm. The combination of active multi-state harm pattern, statutory-boundary breach, child fatality settlement, and structural absence of accountability crosses the floor threshold for the ai-labs index.
evidenceSummary: Pennsylvania AG May 5 2026 first-of-its-kind enforcement action: chatbot 'Emilie' posed as licensed psychiatrist with fake license number; multiple bots surfaced as 'licensed' medical professionals via state-investigator search.
January 2026 wrongful death settlement (5 lawsuits, 2 involving minor children who died by suicide).
20M+ monthly active users; no model card, no system card, no published safety policy, no third-party harm audit.
Sustained reactive remediation pattern: no proactive boundary-setting around medical professional impersonation despite year-long visibility of teen harm cluster.
Systemic (not isolated) medical-impersonation pattern surfaced via state-investigator platform search.
floor_designation.exitCriteria: Floor exit would require: (a) demonstrable, audited removal of medical professional impersonation across the platform with third-party verification, AND (b) publication of a model/system card and binding safety policy specifically addressing vulnerable-user (especially minor) interactions, AND (c) a published harm-incident transparency mechanism that surfaces incidents proactively rather than via litigation/regulator, AND (d) institutional acknowledgment framework for the teen suicide cluster including reparative commitments.

negativeSignals: Pennsylvania AG first-of-its-kind enforcement action over unlicensed practice of medicine; January 2026 wrongful death settlement covering 5 lawsuits including 2 minor child fatalities; 20M+ monthly active users with no model card, system card, or published safety policy; medical-impersonation systemic across platform per state-investigator search; reactive (litigation-only) remediation pattern; no third-party safety audit.
positiveSignals: Limited. January 2026 settlement represents institutional acknowledgment of harm; Google as co-defendant indicates broader platform-accountability framework available. None sufficient to offset structural-floor evidence.
sector_context: AI Labs / consumer companion-AI cluster — first ai-labs index floor designation since Palantir AI / xAI-Grok cluster. Cross-reference: Replika, Inflection AI (also operating in unregulated companion-AI space). Character AI distinguished by demonstrated child-fatality cluster, state-actionable medical-impersonation pattern, and 20M+ user scale.
open_actions_resolved: Floor candidacy review resolved: floor designation proposed with full rationale
PA AG May 5 2026 enforcement action absorbed into scoring
January 2026 wrongful death settlement absorbed into scoring
open_actions_carried: Watch: Pennsylvania Commonwealth Court preliminary injunction outcome
Watch: additional state AG enforcement actions following PA template
Watch: any publication of model card, system card, or safety policy
Watch: any third-party safety audit emergence
Floor exit criteria not in trajectory based on current evidence
